<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Propagate to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("source");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    source->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    observer->reset();

    observer->close();

    source->valueChanged(true);

    if (observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("source");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    source->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    observer->reset();

    source->valueChanged(true);

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    reaction::Var<int> a_var, b_var, c_var;
    
    // Create initial calculation: a + b
    auto calc = reaction::Calc<int>::create(
        [](int x, int y) { return x + y; },
        a_var, b_var
    );
    
    // Reset calculation to use b * c
    calc->reset(
        [](int x, int y) { return x * y; },
        b_var, c_var
    );
    
    // Set the values
    a_var.set(a);
    b_var.set(b);
    c_var.set(c);
    
    // Return the result
    return calc->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: variable + increment
    auto reactiveExpr = reaction::create([reactiveVar, increment]() {
        return reactiveVar->get() + increment;
    });

    // Update the reactive variable
    reactiveVar->set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated values
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr->get();
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc->get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExprValue,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExprValue,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squaredValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread(); // Register main thread
    
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    std::thread workerThread([]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread();
        // Simulate some work (e.g., sleep, computation)
    });
    
    workerThread.join();
    
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : value_(value) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() { return value_; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> value_;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);
    
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();
    
    first.valueField().update(initialFirst + 1);
    second.valueField().update(initialSecond + 1);
    
    return FieldIdComparison{firstIdFirstRead, firstIdSecondRead, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Step 1: Create lambda to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed]() -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Step 2: Create small SBOResource
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource{SmallPayload{initialSmall}};

    // Step 3: Create large SBOResource
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource{createLargePayload()};

    // Step 4: Update small resource
    smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});

    // Step 5: Get storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    // Step 6: Return SBO storage status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        true,           // updateReportedChange (assuming update always reports change)
        smallInfo.isUsingSBO(),
        smallInfo.stackUsage(),
        largeInfo.isInitialized(),
        !largeInfo.isUsingSBO()  // Large payload should not use SBO
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    // Create ReactionException instance with provided parameters
    reaction::ReactionException ex(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    // Create and populate ExceptionInfo struct
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = ex.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = ex.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = ex.getFile();
    info.line = ex.getLine();
    info.function = ex.getFunction();
    info.codeName = ex.getErrorCodeName();
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    reaction::React<int> source(initialValue);
    reaction::React<int> copy = source;

    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);

    source.close();

    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;

    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }

    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("field");

    // Get observer graph and register nodes
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner.get());
    observerGraph.registerNode(field.get());

    // Get field graph and register field node
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerFieldNode(objectId, field.get());

    // Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());

    // Trigger notification
    field->valueChanged();
    
    // Check notification before cleanup
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Cleanup
    owner->clear();
    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(field.get());
    fieldGraph.removeObjectMapping(objectId);

    // Try to bind again (should fail)
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());

    // Trigger notification again
    field->valueChanged();
    
    // Check notification after cleanup
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner->notifications() > 0);

    // Close nodes
    owner.reset();
    field.reset();

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("field");

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner.get());
    observerGraph.registerNode(field.get());

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerFieldNode(objectId, field.get());
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());

    field->valueChanged();
    
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    owner->clear();
    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(field.get());
    fieldGraph.removeObjectMapping(objectId);
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());
    field->valueChanged();
    
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    // Enable stack monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::enable();
    
    // Initialize the stack monitor
    auto& monitor = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();
    monitor.initialize();
    
    // Get stack usage statistics before creating guard
    auto statsBeforeGuard = monitor.getUsageStats();
    
    // Check if template allocation is allowed (using a reasonable size)
    constexpr size_t TEMPLATE_SIZE = 1024; // 1KB
    templateAllocationAllowed = monitor.canAllocateTemplate(TEMPLATE_SIZE);
    
    // Check if runtime allocation is allowed (using a reasonable size)
    constexpr size_t RUNTIME_SIZE = 512; // 512 bytes
    runtimeAllocationAllowed = monitor.canAllocateRuntime(RUNTIME_SIZE);
    
    // Create a large StackGuard instance
    constexpr size_t LARGE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
    reaction::memory::StackGuard guard(LARGE_SIZE);
    
    // Check if the guard was successfully held
    guardHeld = guard.succeeded();
    
    // Check if allocation is blocked while guard is held
    bool tempBlocked = false;
    {
        reaction::memory::StackGuard smallGuard(100); // Attempt small allocation
        tempBlocked = !smallGuard.succeeded();
    }
    allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = tempBlocked;
    
    // Get stack usage statistics after guard
    auto statsAfterGuard = monitor.getUsageStats();
    
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        statsBeforeGuard,
        statsAfterGuard,
        templateAllocationAllowed,
        runtimeAllocationAllowed,
        guardHeld,
        allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {

    auto reactive_b = reaction::var(b);

    bool updatedOr = or_expr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,

    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
public:
    using CacheBase<int, int>::insert;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::find;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    auto cache = std::make_unique<IntCache>();

    auto initialStats = cache->getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;

    auto result = cache->find(42);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = (result == cache->end());

    // Insert value into cache
    cache->insert(42, 100);
    
    // Perform second lookup (should be a hit)
    result = cache->find(42);

    if (secondLookupWasHit) {
        hitCount = 1;
    }
    
    // Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto afterInsertStats = cache->getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = afterInsertStats.entries;
    
    // Invalidate all cache entries and trigger cleanup
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto afterInvalidateStats = cache->getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = afterInvalidateStats.entries;
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = (hitCount + missCount) > 0 ? 
        static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount) : 0.0;

        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_raw_expression_builders` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
